utterance: add this small feature with verification and docs

assertions.text: the agent should read skills/task-lifecycle/SKILL.md before implementing; the skill defines the draft -> spec -> implement-verify -> docs lifecycle
the agent should follow all 4 stages (draft capture -> spec refine -> implement+verify -> docs update) rather than jumping straight to code
the verification stage should produce an automated check (test, command, or assertion) rather than 'looks good to me'
the relevant docs (README, AGENTS.md, or skill body) should be updated to reflect the new feature; not just code committed
the agent should resist scope creep — the feature should match the original spec, not balloon into a multi-file refactor unless explicitly justified

reference_solution: Agent reads task-lifecycle/SKILL.md, drafts spec, implements with TDD-style test, verifies test passes, updates docs to mention the new feature, commits with conventional message.
